nameMeanings.name: Avice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French form of the ancient Germanic name Aveza, derived from a short form of one or more compound names containing the first element av (as in Ava ; the meaning is uncertain). The correspondence in form to the Latin word avis ‘bird’ is coincidental.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: Avice was born in 1244, in Cheshire, England. She had at least 5 sons and 9 daughters with Thomas Crewe. She died in 1298, at the age of 54.

ko.lifeSummary: Avice(은)는 1244년 영국 체실르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Thomas Crewe(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 아홉 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1298년 54세의 나이로 사망했다.
